specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 4.3 The Origin of Purpose: Causal Entropic Forces & Negative Entropy > The Physical Equivalence of Sustainable Ethical Coherence and Causal Path Entropy > 2. A Path Integral Formulation of Causal Path Entropy > 2.1 The State Space and Path Space
source_formulae: P0R03753: 2.1 The State Space and Path Space

P0R03754: Let the total state space of the system described by the SCPN be a high-dimensional manifold, M. A specific, complete microstate of the system at a given instant is represented by a point xM. This point encodes all relevant degrees of freedom across all 15 layers of the architecture. A trajectory, or path, is a continuous map from a time interval [0,tau] to this manifold, denoted as a function x(t).

P0R03755: This function describes the complete evolution of the system's microstate over a future time horizon tau. The set of all such possible future paths, originating from a specific initial state x(0), constitutes the path space, P.
test_protocols: preserve 2.1 The State Space and Path Space source-accounting boundary

specs.section_path: 15-Layer Summary Table > 4.3 The Origin of Purpose: Causal Entropic Forces & Negative Entropy > The Physical Equivalence of Sustainable Ethical Coherence and Causal Path Entropy > 2. A Path Integral Formulation of Causal Path Entropy > 2.1 The State Space and Path Space
source_formulae: P0R03757: For any open thermodynamic system, its evolution is not deterministic but probabilistic, due to interactions with its environment and intrinsic quantum fluctuations. We can therefore define a conditional probability functional,

P0R03759: which represents the probability of the system traversing a specific future path x(t) given that it started at the microstate x(0). This probability functional accounts for all sources of stochasticity and is normalised over the entire path space:
P0R03761: Here, D[x(t)] is the path integral measure, a notational convention for summing over all possible paths in the space P.
